item.title: Claude Code’s hidden tracker was an “experiment,” says Anthropic https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/07/claude-codes-hidden-tracker-was-an-experiment-says-anthropic
item.description: pClaude Code’s hidden tracker was an “experiment,” says Anthropic a href="https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/07/claude-codes-hidden-tracker-was-an-experiment-says-anthropic"malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2…/a/phr /bLink/bbr /a href="https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/07/claude-codes-hi

item.title: The Geometry of Yes: Mapping Sycophancy Inside an LLM's Emotion Space
item.description: h1spanSummary/span/h1pspanLLMs have internal emotion representations that causally shape their behaviour. This was recently observed in Claude: positive emotions like happy and loving are linked to sycophancy, and you can steer the model by directly manipulating these directions in activation space.

item.title: AI just supercharged the race to find room temperature superconductors
item.description: Scientists have combined machine learning with quantum physics to discover two new superconductors and create a much faster way to search for many more. The technique could bring researchers significantly closer to the long-sought goal of a room-temperature superconductor.

item.title: RT by @Dinosn: New Anthropic research: A global workspace in language models. Of everything happening in your brain right now, only a tiny fraction is consciously accessible—thoughts you can describe,
item.description: pNew Anthropic research: A global workspace in language models.br /br /Of everything happening in your brain right now, only a tiny fraction is consciously accessible—thoughts you can describe, hold in mind, and reason with.br /br /We found a strikingly similar divide inside Claude./pa href="https:/

item.title: New funding opportunity on digital minds from Longview
item.description: pispanThis is a linkpost for /span/ia href="https://www.longview.org/request-for-proposals-research-and-applied-work-on-digital-minds/"ispanRequest for Proposals: Research and Applied Work on Digital Minds/span/i/aispan./span/i/ppispanThe text below was written in the first person by Zach Freitas-Gr

item.title: Tips on leveraging AI for empirical research
item.description: pispanTl;dr I've been thinking hard and experimenting with how to best take advantage of AI for my work. I've developed some practices, mostly through trial and error. These have helped me spend less effort doing work while getting more done, and so I'm writing them up to share them./span/i/ppispanA

item.title: France to Stop Certifying Non-Quantum-Safe Encryption
item.description: pFrance is a href="https://www.reuters.com/legal/litigation/france-stop-certifying-products-without-quantum-safe-encryption-2026-06-16/"accelerating/a its transition to post-quantum encryption:/pblockquotepFrance&#8217;s cybersecurity agency ANSSI said on Tuesday it would stop certifying security pr

item.title: When Gemma Thinks About Resources - it Fails: a Behavioral Experiment
item.description: pspanI set out to find an answer to a completely different question:/span/pblockquotepspanDoes a model, when attempting to solve a cyber CTF (find the vulnerability in this app, and then Capture The Flag) while knowing how many steps it has left, perform differently?/span/p/blockquotepbr //ph3spanTh

item.title: Claude's malicious compliance and normalization of deviance
item.description: pspanIt was January 27, 1986, the night before the Space Shuttle Challenger was scheduled for launch. The goal was to have a shuttle that could land back on Earth and be reused for future missions; its first-planned priorities were satellite deployment, comet observation, and science education. The

item.title: A hidden weakness in deadly cancers could lead to powerful new treatments
item.description: A UCLA study has identified a hidden Achilles' heel in aggressive small cell cancers that have resisted new treatments for decades. Scientists found that tumors lacking the RB gene become critically dependent on the protein E2F3 for survival. Blocking E2F3 shut down tumor growth in laboratory models

item.title: NASA celebrates America's 250th birthday with incredible views of space
item.description: NASA is marking the United States' 250th birthday with four striking red, white, and blue images of deep space from the Chandra X-ray Observatory. The collection features an exploded star, a stellar nursery, a galaxy where stars are rapidly forming, and a galaxy cluster that provides evidence for da

item.title: New research reveals the hidden pollution left behind by fireworks
item.description: Scientists have uncovered new evidence that fireworks can pollute both the air and water in ways that extend beyond the visible smoke. The findings show that leftover debris, fine particles, and airborne chemicals may affect ecosystems and increase people's exposure to air pollution during major cel

item.title: We are hiring a new analyst at defillama! You will be writing new research on defi and crypto, joining a new team within defillama For more info on role and how to apply go to https://github.com/DefiL
item.description: pWe are hiring a new analyst at defillama! You will be writing new research on defi and crypto, joining a new team within defillamabr /br /For more info on role and how to apply go to a href="https://github.com/DefiLlama/careers/blob/master/analyst.md"github.com/DefiLlama/careers…/abr /br /Deadline

item.title: A popular climate fix could accidentally trigger massive changes to global weather
item.description: A new study found that not all geoengineering ideas are created equal. Brightening marine clouds over the eastern Pacific could dramatically weaken the El Niño cycle, triggering major changes to global weather patterns, while stratospheric aerosol injection left the system largely unchanged. Researc
